genre_preference.value: mixed (likes thoughtful performances, dislikes poorly executed narratives)
evidence: "Fantastic film - beats the Hunger Games any day of the week."
"The movie showed her in more motels and other people's houses in actually on the Pacific Crest Trail, and was an outright ad for REI..."
"A woman founds a tech startup and the only other woman featured... is her frazzled assistant..."
"It's like a bad stereotype. The premise was a good one but the movie was very, very bad."
"...while it's fast paced, he also breaks it up perfectly in prepping you for each new move..."
"Phenomenal acting well written and terrific cinematography."
"Horrible! Terrible acting - Greg Kinnear cannot save this movie..."
"This is the stuff DeNiro should do more of."
"Kerry Washington was great and I'd love to have seen more of her..."
"...some beautiful touching moments at the end."
"Thoughtful performances that really go deep into the culture and struggles..."
"One of the finest movies I've seen in many many years."

summary: The user shows a strong preference for high-quality films with thoughtful performances and emotional depth, while expressing significant dissatisfaction with poorly executed narratives. They have mixed feelings about genre and pacing, indicating a nuanced taste that values both storytelling and production quality.
